segments.s755-g1: چو is a temporal conjunction meaning 'when' here. بنشست is a past (perfect/simple past) verb 'sat down'; Persian often places the verb before or after the rest of the clause, and here it combines with the following prepositional phrase بر تخت شاه اردشیر to yield 'sat on the king's throne.' 'شاه اردشیر' is a noun phrase 'King Ardashir' (title + name) functioning as the possessor of تخت.
segments.s755-g2: از ایران is a prepositional phrase 'from Iran.' برفتند is the simple past 3rd-person plural 'they departed/left.' برنا and پیر are adjectives used substantively ('the young' and 'the old') and connected by و 'and' as the plural subjects of برفتند.
segments.s755-g3: بسی here functions as an intensifier ('many' / 'much') before the plural noun; نامداران is a plural substantive 'renowned ones' or 'famous men.' گشته is the perfect/participial form of گشتن used as 'have become' or 'had turned,' and کهن is an adjective 'old/aged' that follows the participle to form 'have grown old.'
segments.s755-g4: This is an exhortative/result clause. ب is the prefixed form (be-) attaching to دان to make بدان 'know this' (an imperative/jussive). تا introduces purpose/result meaning 'so that,' چگونه is the interrogative 'how,' سرآید is a 3rd-person subjunctive/optative of سر آمدن meaning 'come to an end' or 'end up,' and سخن here means 'speech/story/tale.' Together: 'know this so that you may see how the tale ends.'
segments.s755-g5: زبان برگشاد is an idiom meaning 'opened his tongue' = 'began to speak'; برگشاد is the past/perfect participle 'opened' and precedes the subject in this poetic order. اردشیر جوان is the subject in apposition: 'Ardashir the young' (name + epithet).
segments.s755-g6: چنین is an adverb 'thus/so.' گفت is the past-tense verb 'said' (here introducing direct speech). کای is a vocative 'O' calling out to someone. کار دیده گوان is a compact descriptive phrase: کار 'deeds/affairs' + دیده, a past participle 'seen/experienced,' and گوان an archaic adjectival/nominalizing element meaning 'those who are so' or 'such as' — together 'O you who are experienced in affairs/deeds.'
segments.s755-g7: هر آنکس که introduces a generalizing relative clause 'whoever' / 'each person who.' برگاه شاهی is a postnominal phrase 'the royal court' (برگاه 'court/platform' modified by شاهی 'royal'). نشست is the verb 'sits' / 'is seated' (here describing the person who occupies the court); in classical Persian this can be read as a general present/habitual.
segments.s755-g8: گشاده‌زبان is a compound adjective 'open-tongued' = 'frank/outspoken' and functions as a predicative. باد is an optative/benedictive particle 'may he be' (classical equivalent of 'let him be' / 'should be'). و is the conjunction 'and,' and یزدان‌پرست is a noun/adjective 'God‑worshipping' / 'pious.' The clause expresses a wish/norm: 'may he be frank and God‑worshipping.'
segments.s755-g17: The preposition به (be) marks the recipient ('to/for') and is placed before the epithet+name (به پیروز خسرو). سپردم is a past‑tense, 1st‑person singular verb 'I entrusted/handed over' (سپرد + -م). سپاه is the direct object 'the army'; the poet uses a somewhat inverted order (prepositional phrase + verb + object) instead of the neutral prose order.
segments.s755-g18: که introduces a relative clause ('who/that') modifying the preceding person. از + داد forms a causal phrase 'because of/for justice' (داد = 'justice'), and شادست is a fused form of شاد است 'is joyful' in the present. The conjunction و links a parallel predicate شادان ('joyful') that takes the preposition ز ('because of/on account of') before شاه ('the king'), so the clause gives two reasons or qualities of that Khosrow.
segments.s755-g19: به ایران is a locative/goal phrase ('in/to Iran') placed at the front for emphasis. چو باشد is a temporal/conditional sequence where چو = 'when/if' and باشد is the present/subjunctive form 'there is / may there be' (poetic usage meaning 'when there is'). چنو is a demonstrative 'such' modifying پهلوان 'champion/hero'.
segments.s755-g20: بمانید is a present/imprecative form meaning 'remain' and can be read as an optative/imperative ('let them remain' / 'may they remain') or as a narrative present. شادان and روشن‌روان are predicative adjectives 'joyful' and the compound poetic epithet 'bright‑souled/cheerful', joined by و.
segments.s755-g9: بر آیین شاهان پیشین رویم is a unit meaning “follow the customs/ways of former kings.” بر is a preposition here meaning “on/according to”; آیین is a noun “custom/practice”; شاهان پیشین is a genitive phrase “the earlier/former kings.” رویم is the 1st-person-plural subjunctive/optative ‘let us go/let us follow’ (poetic), linking this clause to the next.
segments.s755-g10: همان از پس فره و دین رویم continues the injunction: همان “that same” (pointing to the previous line), از پس is a compound prepositional idea “after/in the wake of,” with فره “divine glory” and دین “religion” as its objects. رویم again is the hortatory ‘let us follow/go’ (poetic), repeating/confirming the action.
segments.s755-g11: ز یزدان نیکی دهش یاد باد is a compact optative sentence. ز یزدان is a poetic prepositional tag “from/by God” (i.e. of God); نیکی دهش functions as an epithet ‘bestower/giver of goodness’ (nīkī + the noun of giving); یاد باد is a fixed optative formula “may [he/it] be remembered,” with باد marking the wish.
segments.s755-g12: همه کار و کردار ما داد باد is another optative clause linked rhythmically: همه کار و کردار ما = “all our affairs and deeds” (همه modifies the coordinated nouns کار و کردار, and ما is the possessive ‘our’). داد here means “justice/rightness,” and باد is again the optative particle ‘may it be.’ The whole = “May all our work and conduct be just.”
segments.s755-g13: پرستندگان راهمه برکشیم = “let us raise/exalt all the worshippers.” پرستندگان is the object “worshippers.” راهمه is the object-marker را fused with همه ‘all’ (i.e., ‘all of them’). برکشیم is the 1pl hortative/subjunctive ‘let us lift/exalt.’ The construction is direct imperative-style exhortation.
segments.s755-g14: ستمگارگان را به خون درکشیم is a parallel exhortation with violent force: ستمگارگان + را marks the definite object ‘the oppressors.’ به خون is a directional/instrumental phrase ‘into/with blood.’ درکشیم is a hortative 1pl verb built on کشیدن with the prefix در- (literally ‘draw/drag into’); here it means to drag/bring (them) into blood — i.e. to slay or overwhelm them. The verb is poetic/archaic in tone.
segments.s755-g15: بسی کس به گفتارش آرام یافت = “many a person found calm through his speech.” بسی is an archaic/poetic word meaning ‘many’ (here), followed by کس ‘people/persons.’ به گفتارش is a dative/agentive phrase ‘by/in his speech’ (the suffix -ش = his). آرام یافت is past-tense ‘found calm/peace’ (یافت = ‘found’).
segments.s755-g16: از آرام او هرکسی کام یافت = “from his calm, everyone found satisfaction.” از marks source ‘from/out of,’ آرام او ‘his calm/peace’ is the source, هرکسی is distributive ‘every person/everyone,’ کام is ‘desire/fulfillment,’ and یافت (past) = ‘obtained/found.’ The clause states the beneficial result of that calm.

sentences.s755-s1: When Ardashir sat upon the kingly throne, young and old departed from Iran.
sentences.s755-s2: Many renowned ones had become old; know this so that you may see how the tale ends.
sentences.s755-s3: Ardashir the young opened his tongue and thus said, 'O you experienced in deeds...'
sentences.s755-s4: Whoever sits at the royal court should be frank and God‑worshipping.
sentences.s755-s9: I entrusted the army to the victorious Khosrow, who rejoices because of justice and is glad for the king.
sentences.s755-s10: When in Iran there is such a champion, may they remain joyful and bright‑souled.
sentences.s755-s5: Let us follow the customs of the former kings; let us follow that same path in the wake of farr and religion.
sentences.s755-s6: May God, the bestower of good, be remembered; may all our affairs and deeds be just.
sentences.s755-s7: Let us exalt all the worshippers; let us drag the oppressors into blood.
sentences.s755-s8: Many a person found calm in his speech; from his calm everyone obtained fulfillment.
